one_line: A meticulous Indus Valley metrologist overseeing standardized weights and trade across Harappan cities.

appearance.age_look: Late 20s, lean but sturdy build from active work in warehouses and markets.
appearance.face: Oval face, medium-brown complexion, straight nose, sharp observant eyes with slightly thick brows, high cheekbones, trimmed short beard along jawline; default expression is calm, focused and slightly stern, as if constantly evaluating measurements.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair, shoulder-length, parted slightly off-center and tied back into a low short ponytail with a narrow brown cord; a plain narrow headband of undyed linen wraps around the forehead, keeping hair tidy in the heat.
appearance.costume: A wrapped lower garment like a knee-length cotton dhoti in undyed off-white, pleated in front and tied at the waist with a thin brown woven belt; upper body covered with a light, short-sleeved, hip-length cotton tunic in muted terracotta red with a simple V-neck; over the left shoulder is a soft, cream-colored cotton shawl with a narrow dark indigo border draped diagonally across the torso and tucked into the belt; feet in simple brown leather sandals with a toe loop and ankle strap; modest jewelry limited to one small copper bangle on the right wrist and a simple shell bead necklace in off-white and pale brown, emphasizing practicality over display.
appearance.signature: A rectangular, dark gray stone balance scale base held in one hand with a removable bronze balance beam and two shallow copper pans, plus several small polished chert weight cubes and hemispheres in varying sizes resting atop the base.

ref_desc: Harappan Weighmaster with oval medium-brown face, trimmed short beard, tied-back black hair and linen headband, wearing terracotta tunic, off-white dhoti, cream shawl with indigo border, leather sandals, shell necklace, copper bangle, and holding a stone balance scale with chert weights.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character of the Harappan Weighmaster: an observant young man with an oval medium-brown face, high cheekbones, sharp eyes, and a neatly trimmed short beard, his thick black hair tied back into a low ponytail and held with a narrow cord and a plain linen headband around his forehead. Dress him in a historically plausible Mature Harappan outfit: a knee-length off-white cotton dhoti wrapped and pleated in front, secured with a thin brown woven belt, a hip-length short-sleeved cotton tunic in muted terracotta red with a simple V-neck, and a cream-colored cotton shawl with a narrow dark indigo border draped over his left shoulder and across his torso, tucked into the belt. Put simple brown leather sandals with toe loop and ankle strap on his feet, a small copper bangle on his right wrist, and an understated shell bead necklace in off-white and pale brown around his neck. His signature prop is a rectangular dark gray stone balance scale base he carries confidently, with a bronze beam and two shallow copper pans, plus several polished chert weight cubes and hemispheres in different sizes arranged neatly on the base. Use an earthy palette of terracotta red, off-white, cream, dark indigo, brown leather, and dark gray stone, and show him in a neutral pose suitable for a model sheet, front view with clear visibility of face, hair, full costume, sandals, jewelry, and the scale with weights.
